22.reason: [The condition logic is flawed: both `m <= p->num` and `m >= p->num` can be true when m == p->num, causing the right subtree to be incorrectly updated after already handling the left. This leads to duplicate insertion or incorrect BST structure. It should use `else` instead of `else if (m >= p->num)`.]
